content:
**Seoul, June 4, 2025** — Lee Jae-myung Elected President in Landmark South Korean Election

In a historic and tightly contested election, Lee Jae-myung of the Democratic Party of Korea (DPK) has been elected as the next president of South Korea, following months of political turmoil and the impeachment of former President Yoon Suk Yeol. Lee secured 51.2% of the vote, defeating conservative challenger Han Dong-hoon of the newly formed Reform Conservative Party.

Voter turnout reached a record 81%, reflecting the nation’s heightened interest after the unprecedented events of the past year. “This is a victory for democracy and for the people who demanded change,” President-elect Lee said to supporters gathered in Gwanghwamun Square last night. “I will work to restore trust in our institutions and lead with transparency.”

Lee, the former governor of Gyeonggi Province and 2022 presidential runner-up, ran on a platform focused on economic revitalization, social welfare expansion, and tackling youth unemployment. His campaign capitalized on public frustration with corruption and political infighting, which reached a fever pitch during the impeachment crisis.

International observers praised the election’s transparency and peaceful process. U.S. President Kamala Harris congratulated Lee, saying, “We look forward to deepening our alliance with South Korea under President Lee’s leadership.”

Lee will be sworn in on June 10, inheriting a nation eager for stability and reform.
